ab: yuyuk cu ci kaca, maki’ Sasi’ ka moṟow mu. lunpuw cu patas la ga, masuṟigal min Hori’, maki’ min alang banux nanu’ micuw.
ch: 小時候我們家住在Sasi’，上學以後，就搬到了埔里，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Sasi’. After I started school, we moved to Puli and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: musa’ cu tase la ga, lunpuw cu patas gagaṟux na Itaṟal. kaca ka ba’un mu ci masinpayux ka gagaṟux na Yencumincu.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: kani ka. mahnguw lunpuw abagan pilaw hani. magaluw cu asuṟan mu malikuṟ musa’ min panaṟit ci nabkis alang. mak ba’un min nak tikiy ka gagaṟux na alang min.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: maspat mapgan ka ingkaṟalan naka nabkis hani la. ho’ ga, yona katana’an taxan na.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: ana amol na ke’ ka ba’un su’ sun mu. tahṟuti’ caṟung ka ke’ na Itaṟal ni. ba’ ci ke’ na Seediq, Taluw lu Tanah tunux.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的泰雅語說得很流利，也會說賽德克語、華語和日本語。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Atayal, as well as Seediq, Chinese and Japanese.

ab: panaṟican mu, kaca ka ba’un mu ci saringan yuyuk sunbaba’ ci ke’ na “Tanah tunux.” ka hiya’. Seediq ka kanel ni, hungel ka Taluw kinuba’un ni mumaṟah. kani ka payux la ke’ ba’un ni kumaṟal.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是賽德克族人，而他工作上認識許多華人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Seediq, and he knows many Chinese at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: makakaṟal min ci kaca ga, payux ka gagaṟux na maloṟak wal sikaṟal naka nabkis hani.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: ana min wal ini’ bayi haṟi mung ka ruma’, ho’ ga, wal min pasisingan na nawilung. anak sahyawan mung babuw ni.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: yuyuk cu ci kaca, ini’ mu caṟung bayi ka gagaṟux mu. sikasaṟux mu ka ciragan mu uṟi.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: payux ka kinuba’ mu la ga, tatirahu’ mu galeng na ci’uli’ ka cinragan mu Itaṟal hani,
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為泰雅族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Tayal is unique and something to be proud of.
